heading: Ocean Shores vs Old Adaminaby — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Ocean Shores, New South Wales and Old Adaminaby, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Ocean Shores has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Old Adaminaby (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Ocean Shores or Old Adaminaby safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Ocean Shores has a flood risk score of 31/100, while Old Adaminaby scores 50/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Ocean Shores scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Old Adaminaby scores 65/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Ocean Shores or Old Adaminaby?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Ocean Shores has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.03% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
